speaker: aurora
text: Here sleeps King Cedric, where the Grey is strongest. Only real games will break it.

speaker: aurora
text: Light… at last. I am Queen Aurora. You have woken almost the whole kingdom, brave Caller.

speaker: aurora
text: Only the King still sleeps. To wake him, Pip must cross to the far side and become a queen.

speaker: belle
text: Watch closely — the king and a rook move together. That secret move is called castling!

speaker: belle
text: You learn quickly, Caller. But there is one more thing every player must know…

speaker: belle
text: Mmm… who climbs so high? I am Bishop Belle. Up here we keep the kingdom's SECRET moves.

speaker: belle
text: The mountains shine again! Thank you, Caller — I am wide awake.

speaker: belle
text: Not every game is won, little one. Sometimes a game ends in a tie — a draw.

speaker: belle
text: Beware STALEMATE: if a king can't move but isn't in check, the game is a draw — not a win!

speaker: belle
text: Look — the king has no move, but he is NOT in check. What does that make it?

speaker: belle
text: Castling, en passant, and a pawn becoming a queen — special moves that surprise your foe.

speaker: belle
text: You are ready, Caller. Answer Murk, and the heights are ours.

speaker: cedric
text: Ho! The Grey is lifted and I am awake. Thank you, Caller — you saved the Kingdom of Sixty-Four!

speaker: murk
text: Gah! You actually passed? Fine, fine — the gate is open.

speaker: murk
text: Two friends woken… this is getting out of hand!

speaker: murk
text: My forest! Here I hide my favorite tricks — forks, pins, and skewers. You'll never learn them!

speaker: murk
text: Hmph. Beginner's luck. The high cliffs of Castle Heights will stop you!

speaker: murk
text: Bah — I was SAVING that trick. No fair learning it!

speaker: murk
text: Tee-hee! Wrong square. Find the back row!

speaker: murk
text: Ha! Missed it. Only the knight can reach him now!

speaker: murk
text: Ha! Missed me. You'll have to do better than that!

speaker: murk
text: Stop right there! No one crosses my gate without passing my riddles.

speaker: murk
text: My trickiest riddles yet! Forks, pins, pawns — get them ALL right, or turn back!

speaker: murk
text: Missed! These trees are full of tricks.

speaker: murk
text: Bet you can't beat them all. I'll be watching… and giggling.

speaker: murk
text: Hee-hee! That's not check. Try again, hero.

speaker: murk
text: Hey! Those are MY tricks! …this isn't going how I planned.

speaker: murk
text: No, no, NO! Stay out of my Trickster Forest — it's full of nasty surprises!

speaker: murk
text: Hee! Rushing again? That is how I catch you.

speaker: murk
text: Hee-hee! A pawn playing hero? You'll never wake them all, little Pip.

speaker: murk
text: This is your LAST step, Pip. Play me, level by level… I won't make it easy!

speaker: murk
text: None shall pass the cliff gate without proving they can win AND stay safe!

speaker: murk
text: Hee! My forest fooled you. Look again!

speaker: murk
text: I… I only made trouble because nobody ever played with me. Could… could I stay?

speaker: murk
text: Nope! A pin is a sneaky thing — think harder.

speaker: murk
text: Hee! That's not the mate. Look at f7 again!

speaker: murk
text: Pfft. Knowing the moves and USING them are very different, hero.

speaker: murk
text: One last gate, deep in the trees. Pass it… if you dare.

speaker: murk
text: Worth, schmorth. You'll give away your queen the moment you stop looking!

speaker: murk
text: Riddles about squares, roads, and pieces. Get them right… if you can!

speaker: murk
text: Wrong! Tee-hee… my forest muddles your thinking.

speaker: murk
text: Clever, clever. But my gate on the heights is trickier than the last…

speaker: narrator
text: Stalemate — a tie! The king can't move but is NOT in check. That's a draw, not a win.

speaker: narrator
text: The rook slides in straight lines — like a train on a track!

speaker: narrator
text: Now you get to win for real! Let's practice some easy checkmates you'll use over and over again.

speaker: narrator
text: Spot the Smother. What traps the king?

speaker: narrator
text: How big is the board?. Count the squares.

speaker: narrator
text: Three More Friends. King, knight, and pawn.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate ends the game — the king is in check with no way out!

speaker: narrator
text: Which piece is this?

speaker: narrator
text: There are clever tricks that win you extra pieces! Let's learn the fork and the skewer and start winning.

speaker: narrator
text: The other army answers with a pawn.

speaker: narrator
text: Before you grab a piece, you should check whether it is…

speaker: narrator
text: You did it! The best way to improve is to play and have fun. Pip is so proud of you! 🌟

speaker: narrator
text: Black answers.

speaker: narrator
text: You've learned so much! Now it's time to play me, Pip. Every level can be won, so climb the ladder and try to beat them all!

speaker: narrator
text: Only the knight hops in an L and can leap over pieces.

speaker: narrator
text: A fork is when one piece…

speaker: narrator
text: The bishop leaves f1. Now the squares between king and rook are empty.

speaker: narrator
text: Check! The mighty queen attacks the king.

speaker: narrator
text: This little soldier is the pawn. Tap it! Pawns march straight forward, but capture diagonally. On its first move, a pawn can hop two squares!

speaker: narrator
text: When a pawn reaches the far side, what can it become?

speaker: narrator
text: The Sleeping Bishop.

speaker: narrator
text: Munch munch — all gobbled up! 🍎

speaker: narrator
text: Who's worth the most?. Pick the biggest prize.

speaker: narrator
text: Capture with the rook!. Slide over and grab it.

speaker: narrator
text: When the king is attacked, that's a check, and he must get to safety. If he can't escape at all, that's checkmate and the game is won!

speaker: narrator
text: The black king has only one square to go to — it's almost trapped.

speaker: narrator
text: You did it — a brand new Queen! 👑 Leading with your king is how every pawn gets home. Pip is so proud!

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! Nf7# — the smothered mate. The king is trapped by its own rook and pawns, and a knight check can't be blocked.

speaker: narrator
text: The Four-Move Trap. Guard the f7 square!

speaker: narrator
text: Give a check!. Attack the king in one move.

speaker: narrator
text: Build the Box.

speaker: narrator
text: Tap the rook, then tap a gold star to drive there. Remember — rooks go in straight lines! Collect both stars.

speaker: narrator
text: Paths Light Up.

speaker: narrator
text: An 8×8 grid — 8 rows of 8 squares makes 64!

speaker: narrator
text: Use the queen for checkmate!

speaker: narrator
text: How does a pawn capture?. Pawns are sneaky!

speaker: narrator
text: The other side does the same.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate in two! Your king did the cornering and the rook finished the job.

speaker: narrator
text: Watch the Army Wake Up. The first few moves.

speaker: narrator
text: The Cliff Gate.

speaker: narrator
text: Just a queen and your king now. Use the queen to box Pip's king to the edge, bring your king up to help, and checkmate — don't stalemate!

speaker: narrator
text: Careful stepping! You dodged every red square. 👑

speaker: narrator
text: Bishop's Diagonal Dash. Stay on your color!

speaker: narrator
text: Choo choo! You drove the rook to all four corners. 🚂

speaker: narrator
text: You have a queen against a lonely king. What must you be careful NOT to do?. A. Stalemate the king by accident, turning a win into a tie.. B. Walk your own king away into a brand-new corner square.. C. Check the lonely king over and over, on and on forever.

speaker: narrator
text: Gobble the Apples. Munch them all!

speaker: narrator
text: Punish the Careless Move!. Checkmate in one.

speaker: narrator
text: Quick — how does the rook move?

speaker: narrator
text: The knight jumps in an L-shape to those eight special squares — and can leap over other pieces!

speaker: narrator
text: The queen is worth about 9 — the most of all the pieces!

speaker: narrator
text: The Throne Room.

speaker: narrator
text: Here's the trap in action. Watch the bishop and queen both aim at f7 — and the careless knight move that lets it happen.

speaker: narrator
text: Meet the Rook. The castle tower.

speaker: narrator
text: Which is a good first move?. Center or edge?

speaker: narrator
text: Two pieces at once — they can't both run away!

speaker: narrator
text: The Meadow Blooms.

speaker: narrator
text: Run to safety!. Move your knight out of danger.

speaker: narrator
text: Meet the Queen. The most powerful piece.

speaker: narrator
text: Capture the free rook!

speaker: narrator
text: Meet the Pawn. The brave little soldier.

speaker: narrator
text: Is the king in check?. Is the king under attack?

speaker: narrator
text: Make a fork!. Win the queen with a jump.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate the trapped king with your rook!

speaker: narrator
text: Tap the knight and watch its L-jumps light up. Hop, hop, hop to collect both stars!

speaker: narrator
text: You found a move you like. What's the LAST thing to do before you play it?. A. Close your eyes and move absolutely any piece on the whole board.. B. Just play it instantly and quickly without another single thought.. C. Double-check: does my move leave a piece to be taken for free?

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! King and rook work as a team. You're getting strong!

speaker: narrator
text:
Here's Pip's favorite secret: if a pawn reaches the very last row on the other side, it turns into a QUEEN! 👑

But the enemy king will try to stand in the way. The trick? Your OWN king goes first, like a bodyguard, walking in FRONT of the pawn to clear a path. Then the little pawn follows safely behind, step by step, all the way home. King leads, pawn follows!. Reach the last row and your pawn becomes a queen! 👑. Your KING leads — walk it in front of the pawn.. The pawn follows behind, one square at a time.

speaker: narrator
text: A game that ends with no winner is called a…

speaker: narrator
text: This is the rook. It looks like a little castle tower! Tap it to see where it can go. The rook moves in straight lines — up, down, and across — like a train on a track!

speaker: narrator
text: Here's the secret to checkmate: push the enemy king to the edge of the board, where it has fewer places to run. Then you can trap it!

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate or Stalemate?. Tricky one!

speaker: narrator
text: That's the knight — the horse! It's the trickiest mover, and we'll meet it soon.

speaker: narrator
text: Is the knight safe?. Look for attackers.

speaker: narrator
text: The skewer — win the queen!. Check the king, grab what's behind.

speaker: narrator
text: Can White's queen safely capture the pawn on d5?

speaker: narrator
text: Dodge to the Star. Avoid the red squares!

speaker: narrator
text: The other rook checks on the next row — the ladder climbs!

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate Practice. Find checkmate in one!

speaker: narrator
text: Slide the rook to the back row for mate!

speaker: narrator
text: Level 1 complete! That's a checkmate. On to the next level! 🎉

speaker: narrator
text: The knight leaves g1.

speaker: narrator
text: To checkmate the king, where do you want to push it?. A. Into the middle, where it has lots of room.. B. Right next to your own king to keep it close.. C. To the EDGE or a CORNER, with fewer places to run.

speaker: narrator
text: Every square has a name made of a…

speaker: narrator
text: Check… and Mate!.

speaker: narrator
text: Pawns march straight but capture one square diagonally forward.

speaker: narrator
text: That is promotion! Most players choose a queen, the strongest piece.

speaker: narrator
text: Here's a champion's start: grab the center, get the pieces out, and castle the king to safety. Step through it!

speaker: narrator
text: When your king is under attack, that is called…

speaker: narrator
text: Be a Detective. Look before you move.

speaker: narrator
text: Your bishop is being attacked and can be taken for free next move. What should you do?

speaker: narrator
text: No legal move, but the king is NOT in check. That is…

speaker: narrator
text: Murk's Forest of Tricks.

speaker: narrator
text: Pip's king is trapped! Find checkmate in ONE move to win Level 1.

speaker: narrator
text: They develop their bishop.

speaker: narrator
text: You won the queen! That's a skewer — check the important piece in front, grab the one behind.

speaker: narrator
text: The pawn reaches the end and becomes a QUEEN! A mighty new piece appears.

speaker: narrator
text: Promotion: a pawn's big dream. Reach the end, become a queen!

speaker: narrator
text: The bishop points straight at f7. Sneaky!

speaker: narrator
text: Stalemate means no legal move but the king is NOT in check. That is a…

speaker: narrator
text: White's queen and bishop both attack f7 — checkmate is threatened! What is Black's best defense?

speaker: narrator
text: Sharp as a Sword.

speaker: narrator
text: Which square does the four-move trap attack?

speaker: narrator
text: Black develops too.

speaker: narrator
text: Is it safe to take?. Check the defenders!

speaker: narrator
text: Choo choo! You drove the rook like a train and collected every star!

speaker: narrator
text: Some Pieces Are Bigger Prizes. What's worth the most?

speaker: narrator
text: The enemy rook is sitting there with nothing guarding it. Tap your rook, then capture it!

speaker: narrator
text: The Checkmate Secret. Push the king to the edge.

speaker: narrator
text: Here's the start of a game. Watch a few pieces step out from the back row, ready for fun!

speaker: narrator
text: Every game has a start! Grab the middle of the board, bring your pieces out, and tuck your king somewhere safe.

speaker: narrator
text: Now the queen aims at f7 too. Two attackers, one defender!

speaker: narrator
text: The enemy king wanders.

speaker: narrator
text: Box in the King. King + rook teamwork.

speaker: narrator
text: Free rook! Always look for pieces you can win safely.

speaker: narrator
text: You want to walk your pawn home. Where should your king go?

speaker: narrator
text: Level 4 complete! King and queen is the win you'll use the most. ⭐

speaker: narrator
text: Sneaky Checkmates.

speaker: narrator
text: The rook can't do it alone — march your king up to help push the enemy king to the edge, then checkmate with the rook.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! One rook covers the row below, the other gives check. No escape.

speaker: narrator
text: Let's see what you remember! Answer Pip's questions — get most of them right to win your White Belt!

speaker: narrator
text: Should you trade your queen for a single pawn?

speaker: narrator
text: Always do a quick blunder-check! Before you let go of the piece, make sure your move doesn't hang anything. This habit alone will win you lots of games.

speaker: narrator
text: Your First Real Checkmate. Queen + rook vs. the lonely king.

speaker: narrator
text: A Sneaky Trap.

speaker: narrator
text:
Some checkmates happen because the king is trapped by its OWN pieces! Two famous ones:

The BACK-RANK mate: a king hides behind its three pawns, but if a rook or queen slides to the back row, there's no escape — the pawns block the king's own exit. The SMOTHERED mate: a knight checks a king that's so boxed in by its own pieces it can't move at all. Let's spring both!. A back-rank mate works when the king's own pawns trap it on the edge.. Make 'luft' (a little air) by nudging a pawn so your king can breathe.. A knight's check can't be blocked — deadly against a boxed-in king.

speaker: narrator
text: Hi, I'm Pip! Chess is played on a board of squares, light and dark. Let's get to know it together!

speaker: narrator
text: Promotion! Most players choose a queen, the strongest piece.

speaker: narrator
text: Start Like a Champ. How to begin a game.

speaker: narrator
text: Control the center and develop your knights and bishops — then castle to safety!

speaker: narrator
text: Careful — stalemate is only a tie, even if you have way more pieces!

speaker: narrator
text: Almost There.

speaker: narrator
text: A center pawn marches out and grabs the middle.

speaker: narrator
text: What's the best way to make sure you never lose to the four-move trap?. A. Move only your rook pawns for the first several moves of every single game.. B. Bring your own queen out super early to race the other player to f7 first.. C. Watch the f7 square and meet any threat to it before developing carelessly.

speaker: narrator
text: What is a great way to start a game?

speaker: narrator
text: Pip's Playground.

speaker: narrator
text: The final level! Just a king and a rook. Walk your king up to help the rook trap Pip's king against the edge. You can do it!

speaker: narrator
text: The queen is worth about 9 — the most of all!

speaker: narrator
text: Stalemate is only a tie — careful not to do it by accident!

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! The rook slides over and the king is boxed in with nowhere to run. Your king did the trapping; the rook did the mating.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! The king is IN check (from the rook) and can't escape. That's a win!

speaker: narrator
text: Grab the Middle!. Put a pawn in the center.

speaker: narrator
text: The Deep Forest Gate.

speaker: narrator
text: Watch how your king and rook work together to trap the lonely king in the corner.

speaker: narrator
text: f7 is the weak spot — at the start, only the king guards it!

speaker: narrator
text: The brand-new queen swings across and gives check — that's the power of promotion!

speaker: narrator
text: Drive the lonely king to the edge, then your king helps the queen finish!

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate is when the king is in check and…

speaker: narrator
text: Keep an eye on f7, develop your knights and bishop to guard it (a knight to f6 and the queen or a piece covering f7), and meet the threat before it lands. See it coming, and the trap can never get you.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! Re8# — the rook checks along the back rank and the king's own pawns block every escape.

speaker: narrator
text: Got it! Your rook slid straight up and captured the enemy rook for free.

speaker: narrator
text: Checks and captures are where forks and skewers hide!

speaker: narrator
text: The Kingdom of Grey.

speaker: narrator
text: Check! The king must go back a row.

speaker: narrator
text: Meet the bishop. Tap it to light up its path! The bishop moves in slanted lines, and it always stays on its own color. Sneaky!

speaker: narrator
text: Sometimes Nobody Wins. A tie is called a draw.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate in Two!. A two-step finish.

speaker: narrator
text: It's called 'smothered' because the king is hemmed in by its OWN rook and pawns — it has nowhere to run, and a knight's check can't be blocked or captured. Total trap!

speaker: narrator
text: It's a draw! A lone knight (or a lone bishop) can't force checkmate. You need a rook, a queen, two bishops, or a pawn to promote.

speaker: narrator
text: Early in the game you should…

speaker: narrator
text: Sliders!. Pieces that glide.

speaker: narrator
text: Jump the knight in with check AND attack the queen — a fork!

speaker: narrator
text: Let's Play!. Climb Pip's ladder.

speaker: narrator
text: The black queen is undefended — grab it!

speaker: narrator
text: Walk the king up to the star — but don't step on a red square! Take little steps around them.

speaker: narrator
text: A 'fork' is when:. A. Two of your pawns join up side by side.. B. One piece attacks TWO enemy pieces at once.. C. You move two of your pieces in one turn.

speaker: narrator
text: Back-Rank Mate!. Checkmate in one.

speaker: narrator
text: Keep the King Safe. Check and checkmate.

speaker: narrator
text: Time for a bigger challenge! Answer Pip's questions to earn your Orange Belt.

speaker: narrator
text: The last check. One more look!

speaker: narrator
text: A fork attacks…

speaker: narrator
text: Like e4 — the letter is the column, the number is the row.

speaker: narrator
text: Meet the Bishop. The slanty slider.

speaker: narrator
text: One rook guards the 7th row — the other delivers mate!

speaker: narrator
text: Nim Joins the Quest.

speaker: narrator
text: How many squares are on a chessboard?. A. 64. B. 100. C. 36

speaker: narrator
text: You have a queen AND a rook — loads of power! Push Pip's king to the edge and checkmate. Take your time.

speaker: narrator
text: Rookwell's Rule.

speaker: narrator
text: A Bouncy Knight.

speaker: narrator
text: Not safe! The black pawn on c5 attacks the knight on d4 (pawns capture diagonally). You'd want to move the knight or defend it.

speaker: narrator
text: Other ways to tie. Two more draws.

speaker: narrator
text: Your king is in check from the rook! Move it to a safe square.

speaker: narrator
text: Is it safe to take?. Look before you grab!

speaker: narrator
text: Light or Dark?. What color is the square?

speaker: narrator
text: What is a pin?. Stuck in place.

speaker: narrator
text: Say hello to the knight — the horse! Tap it to see its tricky jumps. The knight hops in an L-shape and is the only piece that can jump over others!

speaker: narrator
text: White to play and checkmate in TWO moves. First, take away the king's last square with YOUR king.

speaker: narrator
text: Not every game has a winner! Sometimes a game ends in a tie, which we call a draw. Let's learn how that can happen.

speaker: narrator
text: Phew! You moved your knight to safety. When a piece is attacked, move it or defend it!

speaker: narrator
text: Check! You must get out of it right away.

speaker: narrator
text: Time to Win!. Your first checkmates.

speaker: narrator
text: Walk your KING up close to take away escape squares. Now the black king is stuck on the edge!

speaker: narrator
text: Can anyone win?. Is there enough to checkmate?

speaker: narrator
text: Walk your pawn to the top row to make a Queen! 👑 Lead with your KING — march it ahead to push the other king out of the way, then bring the pawn home behind it.

speaker: narrator
text: White's pawn advances.

speaker: narrator
text: Which piece moves in an L and can jump over others?

speaker: narrator
text: A pawn that reaches the far end can become a…

speaker: narrator
text: Smothered Mate!. The knight finishes.

speaker: narrator
text: Pip's Final Challenge. Good first moves, forks, pins, and trapping the king.

speaker: narrator
text: Why is castling such a good idea in the opening?. A. It wins a free enemy pawn right away in the opening.. B. It lets your brave king march out into the open and attack.. C. It hides the king safe AND sends a rook to the middle.

speaker: narrator
text: Light and dark, in a checkerboard pattern.

speaker: narrator
text: Trap it on the edge, then your pieces deliver mate.

speaker: narrator
text: Black's pawn leaps two squares, trying to sneak past.

speaker: narrator
text: Right in the middle! Controlling the center is the #1 opening idea.

speaker: narrator
text: Speedy! You found a quick path to the star. ⭐

speaker: narrator
text: Black does the same.

speaker: narrator
text: Your pawn is one step from the end! Tap it and march it to the gold star to make a queen.

speaker: narrator
text: Meet the King. Small steps, big importance.

speaker: narrator
text: This is the king — the most important piece of all. Tap him! The king takes just one little step at a time, in any direction. Keep him safe!

speaker: narrator
text: Push a center pawn (like e4 or d4)! Center pawns open lines for your pieces and grab the most important squares. Corner pawns don't help much early on.

speaker: narrator
text: What is a fork?. Attack two things at once.

speaker: narrator
text: Square Hunt. Find the squares!

speaker: narrator
text: Keep the queen home at first! If she comes out too early, your opponent attacks her and develops their pieces for free while she runs away. Bring out knights and bishops first.

speaker: narrator
text: Level 4: Queen Mate. King + queen vs Pip.

speaker: narrator
text: Don't tie a winning game!. A warning for winners.

speaker: narrator
text: What should you check BEFORE you make your move?. A. Just move as fast as you possibly can and quietly hope it works out.. B. Only ever look at your very own king and nothing else at all.. C. Is anything attacked? Is my move safe? Am I giving a piece away?

speaker: narrator
text: Is the white knight safe where it is?

speaker: narrator
text: Don't Get Back-Ranked. Give your king air.

speaker: narrator
text: Besides stalemate, which of these is also a draw?. A. Capturing the enemy queen with your strong rook for free.. B. Castling to both sides to tuck your king away safely.. C. Both players agree to a draw, or one spot repeats three times.

speaker: narrator
text: A pin means a piece…

speaker: narrator
text: The biggest challenge yet! Ace Pip's questions to earn your Blue Belt and get ready to play.

speaker: narrator
text: Cornered!.

speaker: narrator
text: To checkmate a lonely king, push it to the…

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! Two rooks are the easiest big mate — you climbed the ladder all by yourself!

speaker: narrator
text: Sleeping Friends.

speaker: narrator
text: Rook Star Game. Drive the rook to the stars!

speaker: narrator
text: The rook is on e4, a light square. The board's colors always alternate, like a checkerboard.

speaker: narrator
text: Two Sneaky Mates. Back-rank and smothered.

speaker: narrator
text: Your king leads in FRONT of the pawn, clearing a path and pushing the enemy king aside. The pawn follows safely behind!

speaker: narrator
text: White opens the center.

speaker: narrator
text: Tricks Turned.

speaker: narrator
text: Sneaky Mates Mastered.

speaker: narrator
text: Rooks zoom in straight lines. Tap the rook and collect the star in every corner!

speaker: narrator
text: Knight Star Game. Hop to collect the stars!

speaker: narrator
text: You're a Chess Player!. One last question.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate or Stalemate?. King stuck, but is it check?

speaker: narrator
text: Meet the Knight. The jumping horse.

speaker: narrator
text: The Two-Rook Ladder. Two rooks walk the king to the edge.

speaker: narrator
text: Why castle?. King safety!

speaker: narrator
text: The Queen Awakens.

speaker: narrator
text: Which piece is worth the most points?

speaker: narrator
text: Bring the queen out early?. Careful with the queen!

speaker: narrator
text: Win the Piece! (Practice). Win three in a row to master it.

speaker: narrator
text: Never Fall For It. Pip's safety rule.

speaker: narrator
text: Make a Queen!. Walk the pawn all the way home.

speaker: narrator
text: Move your queen to give the king a check!

speaker: narrator
text: LEVEL 5 COMPLETE! King and rook is a real champion's checkmate. You climbed the whole ladder! 🏆

speaker: narrator
text: Safe! Moving the king is one of the three ways out of check (move, block, or capture).

speaker: narrator
text: Which picture shows how the KNIGHT moves?

speaker: narrator
text: Pip's Second Challenge. Values, check & mate, special moves, and draws.

speaker: narrator
text: The queen is worth the most (9 points)! A rook is 5, a bishop or knight is 3, and a pawn is just 1.

speaker: narrator
text: Be a detective! Every turn, look for attacks (yours and theirs) and make sure your move doesn't leave a piece to be captured for free. This one habit stops most beginner blunders.

speaker: narrator
text: Fork! Nxc7+ checks the king and attacks the queen. After the king moves, take the queen!

speaker: narrator
text: Before each move, ask: 'Will my opponent still have a move?' Leave the king an escape square until you actually checkmate.

speaker: narrator
text: A pawn marches forward to grab the middle of the board.

speaker: narrator
text: A bishop slides out along a diagonal. The game has begun!

speaker: narrator
text: A 'pin' is when:. A. You push a pawn all the way home to make a brand-new queen.. B. A piece can't move — something bigger sits right behind it.. C. You attack only the enemy king with a sudden check.

speaker: narrator
text: Trick Practice. Win pieces! Get 3 to master.

speaker: narrator
text: The Wise Way.

speaker: narrator
text: Level 3 complete! You played it out and won with your big army. 💪

speaker: narrator
text: Level 5: Rook Mate. The toughest level!

speaker: narrator
text: To find tricks, always look for…

speaker: narrator
text: The king is pushed to the edge.

speaker: narrator
text: Watch the Trap Spring. See how it works.

speaker: narrator
text: A pawn that reaches the end turns into a Queen (you can even pick a rook, bishop, or knight — but the queen is strongest!). That's why walking a pawn home can win the game.

speaker: narrator
text: Level 2: Pawn Race!. Promote to win.

speaker: narrator
text: Hop hop — you collected every star! The knight's jumps are tricky, and you've got them.

speaker: narrator
text: The black king has no move. Is this Checkmate or Stalemate?

speaker: narrator
text: No! The knight on f6 is guarding the pawn. If the queen takes, the knight takes the queen back — losing a 9 for a 1. Always check what defends a piece first.

speaker: narrator
text: Yum! Tap the knight and hop onto every apple to gobble it up.

speaker: narrator
text: Clever Tricks. Win pieces with a trick!

speaker: narrator
text: Move it (or defend it)! When a piece is attacked and would be lost for free, don't just leave it — slide it to a safe square or protect it.

speaker: narrator
text: To checkmate with a king and queen, you push the enemy king to the…

speaker: narrator
text: Game Time!. Just for fun.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! The queen takes f7, guarded by the bishop. Game over in four moves.

speaker: narrator
text: Stop the Trap!. How does Black survive?

speaker: narrator
text: Bring the queen next to the king — your king guards her!

speaker: narrator
text: Before you move. Pip's checklist.

speaker: narrator
text: The queen is the most powerful — she slides straight like a rook and diagonally like a bishop!

speaker: narrator
text: You have a queen AND a rook — tons of power! Use them to push the king to the edge (like a ladder) and checkmate. Take your time.

speaker: narrator
text: Pip's big dream: become a Queen!. Your king is the pawn's bodyguard.

speaker: narrator
text: Your piece is attacked!. What now?

speaker: narrator
text: The white pawn reaches the 5th row.

speaker: narrator
text: Castling is the only move where TWO pieces move at once — the king and a rook. It pops your king somewhere safe. Watch!

speaker: narrator
text: What is checkmate?

speaker: narrator
text: If it's defended, taking it could lose your own piece!

speaker: narrator
text: Special Tricks. Three fun extra moves.

speaker: narrator
text: A pawn moves straight ahead. But how does it CAPTURE?

speaker: narrator
text: Queen check!. Use the queen this time.

speaker: narrator
text: Which piece is tall with a cross on top?

speaker: narrator
text: A fork attacks two pieces at once — the knight is the champion forker! Your opponent can only save one.

speaker: narrator
text: Bring the queen close — your king guards her!

speaker: narrator
text: What does a pawn become?. The happy ending.

speaker: narrator
text: A knight hops out in an L-shape.

speaker: narrator
text: White has only a king and ONE knight. Black has only a king. Can anyone checkmate?

speaker: narrator
text: Green Returns to the Meadow.

speaker: narrator
text: Where should your king go?. Lead the way!

speaker: narrator
text: Black makes a quiet move.

speaker: narrator
text: Win with King and Rook. A little trickier.

speaker: narrator
text: Win the free piece!. This one really is free.

speaker: narrator
text: A draw is a tie — nobody wins.

speaker: narrator
text: They develop a knight too.

speaker: narrator
text: Stalemate — a tie! The king has no move but is NOT in check. Be careful not to do this when you're winning!

speaker: narrator
text: Win with King and Queen. The most common checkmate.

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate in One!. Find the mate. Win 4 to master!

speaker: narrator
text: In a smothered mate, what stops the checked king from escaping?

speaker: narrator
text: Welcome to my arcade! Tap a piece and zoom around the board to collect the stars. Play as many times as you like!

speaker: narrator
text: A pin freezes a piece: if it moved, it would expose something bigger (like the king or queen) behind it.

speaker: narrator
text: Here comes the queen — the strongest piece of all! Tap her. The queen moves in straight lines AND slanted lines. She can zoom almost anywhere!

speaker: narrator
text: Grab the free piece!. It's not guarded — take it!

speaker: narrator
text: Rook Roundup. Zoom to the corners!

speaker: narrator
text: You made a queen! Getting a pawn to the far side is a super power.

speaker: narrator
text: Make a Queen!. March the pawn home.

speaker: narrator
text: Eight rows of eight — that makes 64 squares!

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! King and queen is the win you'll get the most. Great job!

speaker: narrator
text: The rook moves in straight lines. The slanted lines are for the bishop, and the jumps are for the knight!

speaker: narrator
text: Before every move, be a chess detective! Look at what's being attacked and make sure your move is safe, so you don't give pieces away.

speaker: narrator
text: Qe7 brings a second guard to f7, so the queen can't crash in. Now Black is fine — and White's queen is out too early and can be chased. Always meet the threat on f7!

speaker: narrator
text: Is the black king in CHECK right now?

speaker: narrator
text: Your First Real Mates.

speaker: narrator
text: A great first move grabs the…

speaker: narrator
text: Move your rook to give the king a check!

speaker: narrator
text: You beat every level! What's the best way to get even better now?. A. Never play another game of chess again.. B. Play lots of games with friends and family!. C. Hide the chess set away in a closet.

speaker: narrator
text: Get the rook to the star in just 3 hops! Remember — rooks go in straight lines.

speaker: narrator
text: In check with no escape = checkmate. Game over!

speaker: narrator
text: Two rooks make a ladder — deliver mate!

speaker: narrator
text: Pip's First Challenge. Squares, colors, and how the pieces move.

speaker: narrator
text: The Grey Roads.

speaker: narrator
text: Uh oh — your knight is being attacked! Tap it and hop it to the safe gold star.

speaker: narrator
text: On every turn, what's the best habit for finding tricks?. A. Only watch your own king the whole game.. B. Move quickly and don't look at the board.. C. Look at every check and capture you can make.

speaker: narrator
text: Long Live the Kingdom!.

speaker: narrator
text: The king takes tiny steps, the knight hops in an L, and the pawn marches ahead. Let's meet them all!

speaker: narrator
text: The Square Kingdom Blooms.

speaker: narrator
text: A pawn opens a path for the bishop.

speaker: narrator
text: How do you keep YOUR king safe from a back-rank mate?. A. Push all three pawns in front of your king as far up the board as you can.. B. March your king up into the middle of the board to keep it far from the edge.. C. Nudge a pawn near your king to give it an escape square ('make luft').

speaker: narrator
text: Another piece out — the bishop aims across the board.

speaker: narrator
text: Can the white queen safely capture the pawn on d5?

speaker: narrator
text: When your pawn reaches the very last row, what can it turn into?. A. It disappears off the board.. B. A Queen (or another big piece) — the most powerful piece! 👑. C. Nothing — it just stops there.

speaker: narrator
text: Back-rank mate — the king is stuck behind its pawns!

speaker: narrator
text: Smooth slider! The bishop stayed on the dark squares the whole way. ⛪

speaker: narrator
text: It's the very first move. Which plan is better?

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate or Stalemate?. King in check with no escape?

speaker: narrator
text: It's White's turn. The enemy rook is on your rook's line with nothing guarding it. Tap your rook, then tap the green dot to capture it!

speaker: narrator
text: Check the king so it steps aside, then grab the queen behind it — a skewer!

speaker: narrator
text: Black mirrors.

speaker: narrator
text: How to spot tricks. Pip's top tip!

speaker: narrator
text: Check the king so it steps aside, then win the queen behind it (a skewer).

speaker: narrator
text: A fork attacks two things at once — they cannot both run away!

speaker: narrator
text: A careless move — it does NOT defend f7!

speaker: narrator
text: Is the rook standing on a LIGHT square or a DARK square?

speaker: narrator
text: When Nobody Wins.

speaker: narrator
text: Trap Defused.

speaker: narrator
text: Castle Heights Blooms.

speaker: narrator
text: Super hopping! You guided the knight all over the board. 🐴

speaker: narrator
text: A Great Way to Start. Watch a perfect opening.

speaker: narrator
text: Check! Your rook attacks the king. Now the king has to escape.

speaker: narrator
text: A Spark of Color.

speaker: narrator
text: It's stuck — moving it would expose a bigger piece behind it.

speaker: narrator
text: Castling: tuck your king away. King and rook move together.

speaker: narrator
text: Your king covers the escape — bring the queen in for mate!

speaker: narrator
text: Which piece can jump over other pieces?

speaker: narrator
text: Which Piece?. Name that piece.

speaker: narrator
text: Murk's Gate.

speaker: narrator
text: En passant: the sneaky capture. A tricky pawn rule.

speaker: narrator
text: When your pawn reaches the other end, what can it turn into?. A. Only a strong new queen, and nothing else.. B. A queen, rook, bishop, or knight — your choice!. C. A brand new king to help the old one.

speaker: narrator
text: Push the king to the edge! In the middle it has 8 escape squares; in the corner it has only 3. Trapping is much easier at the edge.

speaker: narrator
text: The checkmate secret. Where do you trap the king?

speaker: narrator
text: En passant! White grabs it 'in passing', landing on d6. This only works right away!

speaker: narrator
text: What can a pawn become?. Choose wisely.

speaker: narrator
text: Never give up your strongest piece for a weak one!

speaker: narrator
text: The black king is smothered — boxed in by its own rook and pawns. Only a knight can reach it. Mate in one!

speaker: narrator
text: Black just played a careless knight move and left f7 undefended. Deliver checkmate in one!

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! Qxf7# — the queen captures, the bishop defends her, and the king can't escape. That's the four-move trap!

speaker: narrator
text: Straight lines, up/down and across!

speaker: narrator
text: Checkmate! With a queen and rook it's like a ladder — one piece checks, the other guards. You did it!

speaker: narrator
text: Get a piece out! The knight develops and eyes the center.

speaker: narrator
text: Should you bring your queen out to attack on the very first moves?. A. Yes, send her out on the very first move only.. B. No — they'll chase her around, get pieces out for free.. C. Yes — she's the strongest, so attack with her right away!

speaker: narrator
text: Check the king — when it steps aside, the queen behind it is yours!

speaker: narrator
text: Use your queen to push the king to the EDGE, then bring your king up to help. Then checkmate — but don't stalemate!

speaker: narrator
text: It retreats.

speaker: narrator
text: How does the knight move?. Spot the L-jumps.

speaker: narrator
text: The black queen is undefended — take it!

speaker: narrator
text: Knight's Hop Tour. Hop to every star!

speaker: narrator
text: How many squares are on a chess board?

speaker: narrator
text: When you capture an enemy piece, you take it off the board! But some pieces are worth more than others, so grab the big ones safely.

speaker: narrator
text: You pick: queen, rook, bishop, or knight. Almost always choose the queen — she's the strongest!

speaker: narrator
text: How does the rook move?. Pick the right arrows.

speaker: narrator
text: The black king is trapped behind its own pawns. Slide your rook to the back rank for checkmate!

speaker: narrator
text: Star in 3 Hops. Can you do it fast?

speaker: narrator
text: Champions' Road Blooms.

speaker: narrator
text: Giving your king a little 'luft' (air) — like nudging the h-pawn one square — means a check on the back rank isn't mate, because your king has somewhere to step. A handy habit once your rooks and queen are off guarding home.

speaker: narrator
text: A Pawn's Big Dream.

speaker: narrator
text: Develop your pieces and castle your king to safety.

speaker: narrator
text: The bishop only moves on slanted lines and stays on its own color. Slide up the big diagonal to grab every star!

speaker: narrator
text: Get out of check!. Your king is under attack!

speaker: narrator
text: The king wears the cross — keep him safe!

speaker: narrator
text: Level 1: Beat Pip in One!. One move to win.

speaker: narrator
text: How does the rook move?

speaker: narrator
text: Welcome to the Board. Where chess happens.

speaker: narrator
text: A knight comes out on the other side too.

speaker: narrator
text: Castling! The king jumps two squares to g1 and the rook hops over to f1. Safe and snug.

speaker: narrator
text: The black queen is sitting on a square nothing defends. Tap your queen and grab it!

speaker: narrator
text: Which piece can move both straight AND diagonally?

speaker: narrator
text: Only the knight can hop over pieces, in its L-shape!

speaker: narrator
text: My number-one tip: before you move, look at all the CHECKS and CAPTURES. That's how you find forks and skewers!

speaker: narrator
text: 'En passant' means 'in passing'. If an enemy pawn zooms past yours using its two-square jump, your pawn can grab it as if it moved only one square!

speaker: narrator
text: Two rooks can checkmate all by themselves! One rook checks, the other fences the king in. Watch the 'ladder'.

speaker: narrator
text: Black copies — but doesn't notice the danger.

speaker: narrator
text: Castling does two great things at once: your king hides safely behind its pawns, and a rook jumps toward the center where it's useful. Try to castle early in every game!

speaker: narrator
text: How does a pawn capture an enemy piece?

speaker: narrator
text: Into Battle Meadow.

speaker: narrator
text: 64 squares — 8 across and 8 down. Half light, half dark!

speaker: narrator
text: Amazing — you found them all! Knowing square names helps you talk about chess.

speaker: narrator
text: Chess squares come in two colors. Which two?

speaker: narrator
text: The enemy rook is free — slide up and grab it!

speaker: narrator
text: Careful and Strong.

speaker: narrator
text: Jump the knight in with check AND attack the queen (a fork)!

speaker: narrator
text: Level 2 complete! Your pawn became a queen. 👑

speaker: narrator
text: Free queen! You checked that it was undefended first — that's playing smart.

speaker: narrator
text: Players can agree to a draw, and the same position repeating three times is a draw too — often from endless checks (perpetual check).

speaker: narrator
text: If a pawn reaches the far end of the board, it turns into any piece you choose — almost always a powerful queen!

speaker: narrator
text:
There's a famous trap called Scholar's Mate. The attacker points a bishop and the queen at one square — f7 — because at the start of the game only the king guards it.

If you're not watching, it's checkmate in four moves! But it's easy to stop once you know the secret: guard f7 and don't let the queen come crashing in. Let's learn to see it, stop it, and turn it around.

speaker: narrator
text: Jump your knight in with check AND attack the queen at the same time!

speaker: narrator
text: The black king can't move. Is this Checkmate or Stalemate?

speaker: narrator
text: It's a trap! The black pawn on e6 guards d5. If the queen takes, the pawn takes the queen back — giving away your best piece for a pawn. Always check what's defending before you capture.

speaker: narrator
text: Pawns capture one square DIAGONALLY forward — not straight ahead. Tricky!

speaker: narrator
text: The rook, the bishop, and the queen love to slide across the board. Let's see where each one can go!

speaker: narrator
text: The knight loves to hop! Tap the knight and jump in L-shapes to collect all the stars.

speaker: narrator
text: Race your pawn to the top of the board to make a queen and win! Tap the pawn and march it home.

speaker: narrator
text: Control the center — it gives your pieces the most power!

speaker: narrator
text: Level 3: Big Power Mate. Queen + rook vs Pip.

speaker: narrator
text: Which piece is worth the MOST points?

speaker: narrator
text: Which picture shows how the ROOK moves?

speaker: narrator
text: Chess has a few special moves! You can tuck your king away, turn a pawn into a queen, and even make a sneaky pawn capture.

speaker: narrator
text: Yes! The white rook attacks the king straight up the e-file. When your king is in check, you MUST get it to safety.

speaker: narrator
text: Win with Two Rooks. Do the ladder yourself!

speaker: narrator
text: Secrets Unlocked.

speaker: narrator
text: Castle! The king zips to the corner, safe and sound. That's a perfect opening: center, pieces out, king safe.

speaker: narrator
text: Use the ladder! One rook checks, the other holds a row, and walk the king to the edge for checkmate.

speaker: narrator
text: The middle of the board is the best place to be! Tap your pawn and march it two squares into the center.

speaker: narrator
text: They Stir!.

speaker: nim
text: Boing! Watch closely, Caller — see how the bishop and queen gang up on f7!

speaker: nim
text: Boing! Boing! Hi-hi-hi! I'm Nim the knight — fastest hopper on Champions' Road!

speaker: nim
text: Ready for my favorites, Caller? Mates where the king is trapped by its OWN pieces!

speaker: nim
text: This is my favorite, Caller — the king's boxed in by its own friends. Hop in!

speaker: nim
text: To catch a king, build a BOX! Take away his squares one by one until he's stuck.

speaker: nim
text: We've got this, Caller. Show Murk how sharp you are!

speaker: nim
text: Which way do we chase the king, Caller? Toward the edge — pick it!

speaker: nim
text: The back-rank mate and the smothered mate — sneaky and beautiful. Let's spring them!

speaker: nim
text: Wanna win? START strong! Grab the middle, wake your pieces up, keep your king cozy.

speaker: nim
text: They left f7 wide open, Caller — finish it in one move!

speaker: nim
text: Boing! You spotted it AND turned it around. Four-move traps don't scare us!

speaker: nim
text: Ooh, you're GOOD. Can I come with you? I wanna help wake everyone too!

speaker: nim
text: Back-rank AND smothered — you've got a whole bag of checkmates now!

speaker: nim
text: Boom! Mate! The whole road is sparkling again. Three friends awake!

speaker: nim
text: That's YOUR dream, Pip! Let's practice walking pawns home, step by step.

speaker: nim
text: One move, two targets — that's a FORK! Find it, Caller!

speaker: nim
text: Oh-ho, a FORK hits two things at once! We can use Murk's own tricks against him!

speaker: nim
text: They gang up on one square — f7. Learn to see it, and nobody can ever catch you with it!

speaker: nim
text: The king's stuck behind its pawns, Caller — slide in for mate!

speaker: nim
text: Boxed him in! You're a natural king-catcher, Caller!

speaker: nim
text: A skewer pokes through one piece to grab the big one behind it. Go!

speaker: nim
text: Psst, Caller — some tricksters try to win in just FOUR moves! It's called Scholar's Mate.

speaker: nim
text: Watch the two rooks climb like a ladder, pushing the king to the edge!

speaker: nim
text: Two rooks, a queen, a single rook — each one can checkmate a lonely king. Let's learn the patterns!

speaker: nim
text: Boing! Grab the middle of the board — that's how champions begin!

speaker: pip
text: Of course, Nim! Now we're a team of three — me, Nim, and you, Caller!

speaker: pip
text: Castling AND turning a pawn into a queen? These are wonderful!

speaker: pip
text: We'll show Murk what we can do. Ready, Caller?

speaker: pip
text: Show the Grey what we've learned, Caller. Find the checkmate!

speaker: pip
text: Down to Champions' Road, Caller. I hear something fast and bouncy down there…

speaker: pip
text: Learn the roads with me, and we can reach anyone who needs us!

speaker: pip
text: Look, Caller! The whole Square Kingdom has its colors back!

speaker: pip
text: More color is coming back. The kingdom is waking up, bit by bit!

speaker: pip
text: Surprises don't scare us anymore. Into the forest, team!

speaker: pip
text: Every square has a secret name, Caller. Find the ones I call out and the kingdom listens!

speaker: pip
text: I looked before I leaped — and nothing got taken for free!

speaker: pip
text: Did you see? They wiggled! Learning their moves is waking them up.

speaker: pip
text: This part is close to my heart, Caller — help me find where a pawn becomes a queen!

speaker: pip
text: That's Murk, the trickster magpie. Don't worry — we'll prove him wrong, Caller!

speaker: pip
text: Each one moves in its own special way. If we learn how they move, we can wake them!

speaker: pip
text: Secret moves?! Teach us every one, Belle!

speaker: pip
text: To wake the sleeping pieces, we must travel the kingdom's roads.

speaker: pip
text: We've learned so much, Caller. Answer Murk's questions and the gate will open!

speaker: pip
text: You found them all! See — a little more color already.

speaker: pip
text: Everything we've learned leads here. Checkmate, Caller!

speaker: pip
text: Like herding a sheep into a pen. Let's practice the trap, Caller!

speaker: pip
text: We cleared the playground! Our moves are quick now.

speaker: pip
text: Let's master every trick, Caller — and find who's sleeping deep in these trees.

speaker: pip
text: Next, Caller — the great king-and-queen and king-and-rook mates. Let's finish the road!

speaker: pip
text: Of course, Murk. Every kingdom needs a trickster friend. We did it, Caller — together!

speaker: pip
text: Walk the king to the edge, then snap the trap shut. You can do it, Caller!

speaker: pip
text: I'm Pip, the smallest pawn. And YOU are my Caller. Together we can bring the colors back!

speaker: pip
text: Now I can see the roads glowing! Straight ones and slanted ones.

speaker: pip
text: I reached the far side… I'm a QUEEN! The crown is mine!

speaker: pip
text: Everyone is fast asleep. The Grey put them to sleep — even the King!

speaker: pip
text: First I must learn this place. Help me explore the board of 64 squares!

speaker: pip
text: Oh… where are all the colors? The whole kingdom turned grey!

speaker: pip
text: Forks, pins, skewers — they're OURS now!

speaker: pip
text: Every pawn made it home! My own dream feels closer than ever.

speaker: pip
text: Our first friend! Come on — Battle Meadow is waiting!

speaker: pip
text: Teach us, Sir Rookwell! Help us learn what each piece is worth, Caller.

speaker: pip
text: Some roads go straight — up, down, and across. Some go slanted, like a diagonal.

speaker: pip
text: The grass is turning green! We know what the pieces are worth now.

speaker: pip
text: The far side… my dream! Come on, team — to Pip's Arena!

speaker: pip
text: Now let's turn those traps into real checkmates.

speaker: pip
text: So I must be careful NOT to stalemate when I'm winning. Got it!

speaker: pip
text: Then let's practice, Caller — to the playground!

speaker: pip
text: Look! When we learned the squares, a tiny bit of color came back!

speaker: pip
text: Time to play! These games make our moves quick and strong.

speaker: pip
text: But up ahead I see a big grey gate, and Murk is sitting on it…

speaker: pip
text: Here are three sleepy friends: the King, the Knight, and another little pawn like me.

speaker: pip
text: Watch closely, Caller — the sleepy pieces are stretching awake and stepping out!

speaker: pip
text: Everything we learned comes down to this. Play with me, Caller — all the way to the crown!

speaker: pip
text: Up we climb, Caller — someone is sleeping on the heights, and we'll wake her too!

speaker: pip
text: Good first moves! Show us, Caller — let's open like champions.

speaker: pip
text: This part is close to my heart, Caller. A pawn who marches all the way home becomes a QUEEN.

speaker: rookwell
text: Attack the enemy king — give check!

speaker: rookwell
text: When the king is attacked, that's CHECK — he must escape at once.

speaker: rookwell
text: Checkmate! You did it. Battle Meadow is green and golden once more.

speaker: rookwell
text: A brave piece is also a CAREFUL one. Before every move, look: is anything in danger?

speaker: rookwell
text: When he's attacked and CAN'T escape — that's checkmate. That's how chess is won!

speaker: rookwell
text: …Who woke me? I am Sir Rookwell. Beyond this gate, in Battle Meadow, I will teach you to be brave.

speaker: rookwell
text: But some pieces are worth more than others. A wise fighter knows what to trade.

speaker: rookwell
text: Before you leap, look for danger. Is this knight safe where it stands?

speaker: rookwell
text: Murk loves it when you forget to check. Let's not give him the chance.

speaker: rookwell
text: Well done, Caller. Now for the most important move of all… the checkmate.

speaker: rookwell
text: Some pieces are worth more than others, Caller. Tap the most valuable one!

speaker: rookwell
text: Welcome to Battle Meadow! Here, pieces can bump enemies off the board. That's a capture!
